title: optimization searching stops too early

But since it only explores in the very beginning it very eraly locks up in a local maxima/minima that is far from what is achieveable.

title: Improve curve control in optimization tool.
description:
Based on the visual evidence from the 'Takeoff' videos where control points "float" off the curve, the spline type being used is a Non-Uniform Rational B-Spline (NURBS), likely of Cubic (Degree 3) order.

The "offset" you observe is technically called the Control Polygon (or Control Hull).

Why this "Offset" Control is Critical for Optimization
In standard "Interpolated Splines" (like simple CAD splines), the line is forced to pass through every dot you place. This is bad for hydrofoils because:

It creates "Wiggles": If you move one point slightly up, the line has to "swerve" aggressively to hit it and then swerve back. This creates invisible curvature spikes that cause cavitation.

Global Distortion: Moving one point often ripples changes down the entire length of the foil.

The "Takeoff" Approach (B-Splines)
In the B-Spline method shown:

The Magnets: Think of the control points not as "anchors" the line must touch, but as "magnets" that pull the line towards them.

The Hull Property: The curve is mathematically guaranteed to stay inside the shape formed by connecting the control points (the Convex Hull).

Smoothness: Because the curve doesn't have to hit the points, it acts like a stiff, bent ruler. It naturally "smooths out" any jittery inputs from the optimizer or the user.

Recommendation for Flow5 Implementation
For the development team, specify the following B-Spline Interaction Model:

Degree: Fixed at 3 (Cubic).

Visuals: Draw the "Control Polygon" (dashed lines connecting the offset points) so users can see the "hull" they are manipulating.

Clamp the Ends: The first and last control points must overlap with the curve endpoints (clamped knots) to ensure the Leading Edge and Trailing Edge are exactly where the user sets them.
close_reason: Implemented V3_BSpline_Control preset with approximating B-spline control points

title: Foil optimization in Flow5 (end-to-end)
description: Source of truth: FOIL_OPTIMIZATION_PLAN.md (docs/ are inspiration only).\n\nGoal: ship a complete foil optimization workflow (XDirect UI → optimization task → XFoil evaluation → results → apply/save) with headless API smoke tests.

title: FoilOptim: robust calcFitness penalties + polar-type handling
description: Make PSOTaskFoil::calcFitness deterministic and failure-tolerant: consistent penalties for invalid geometry vs non-convergence, correct handling for polar types (fixed AoA/Cl/etc), and clear objective-to-metric mapping.\n\nSee FOIL_OPTIMIZATION_PLAN.md Phase A.

title: FoilOptim: headless PSO run harness (deterministic small run)
description: Provide a minimal headless runner (under API_examples/) that runs a small PSO loop for a few iterations and reports best fitness + improvement vs baseline. Should be deterministic-ish (fixed seed / stable config) and support cancellation.\n\nSee FOIL_OPTIMIZATION_PLAN.md Phase C.

title: FoilOptim: headless smoke/regression tests
description: Maintain API_examples-based smoke/regression coverage for foil optimization paths (converged baseline, invalid geometry, XFoil unconverged, cancellation).\n\nSee FOIL_OPTIMIZATION_PLAN.md Phases A/F.
close_reason: 3/4 test paths implemented (baseline, invalid geometry, unconverged). Cancellation test (hmi.3.4) deferred to P3 due to headless threading limitations.

title: FoilOptim Test: baseline converged fitness (finite metrics)
description: Headless smoke test should assert: converged=true and finite objective metrics for a known foil+polar baseline. (The existing API_examples/foiloptimize harness covers this; keep it stable and tighten assertions as needed.)

title: FoilOptim Test: invalid geometry → penalty path
description: Add a headless case that generates invalid foil geometry (or violates constraints) and assert calcFitness returns a penalty + converged=false (or equivalent contract).

title: FoilOptim Test: XFoil unconverged → penalty path
description: Add a headless case that triggers XFoil non-convergence (or uses extreme parameters) and assert calcFitness returns the expected penalty contract without crashing.

title: FoilOptim Test: cancellation path
description: Test 5 added but SKIPPED in headless mode - cancellation requires Qt event loop for async thread coordination. Can be tested manually in GUI or needs alternative approach (signal-based mock).

title: FoilOptim Bug: OpenBLAS dgetrf_parallel segfault in spline solve
description: Observed SIGSEGV in libopenblas dgetrf_parallel during CubicSpline::solve() via matrix::solveLinearSystem → Foil::initGeometry (seen in gdb backtrace).\n\nMitigation: run headless tests with OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1; long-term fix may require forcing single-thread LAPACK path or switching solver.
notes: Repro: OptimFoilDlg Run -> 'Building swarm...' then app crashes (progress bar in busy/full state). Crash occurs during swarm build before OPTIM_MAKESWARM_EVENT, likely inside Foil::initGeometry -> CubicSpline::solve -> OpenBLAS dgetrf. Investigate: validate reduced base-node set ordering/duplicates; consider keeping full base nodes with y-delta updates instead of replacing base nodes; add guards/logging around spline solve and geometry validity; evaluate pop size/variable count impact.

title: FoilOptim: cache evaluation results (hash particle positions)
description: Add optional caching for expensive XFoil evaluations (hash particle position vector → computed metrics) to avoid repeat work during PSO.

title: FoilOptim: multi-threading safety audit (XFoilTask globals)
description: Audit whether PSO fitness evaluation can be parallelized safely (XFoilTask/XFoil globals, cancellation flags, shared state). Gate multi-threading if not safe.

comments.author: johe2
comments.text: Audit Update: Identified that XFoilTask uses static s_IterLim and s_bCancel. This limits thread safety for concurrent tasks requiring different configs or independent cancellation. For FoilOptim, we mitigate by setting/restoring s_IterLim on the main thread, assuming single optimization task. PSOTaskFoil now uses deep copies of Foil/Polar to avoid pointer races.

title: Windows build + test workflow
description:
Define and validate a Windows 11 build + test workflow (MSVC Qt6 kit) for Flow5 + FoilOptim.

Scope:
- Document prerequisites (Qt6 MSVC kit, Visual Studio, SDKs: Gmsh headers/libs, OpenCascade if enabled).
- Add scripts or notes for building the qmake project (flow5.pro) on Windows and running the foiloptimize example.
- Optional: set up CI job (later) or checklist.

Acceptance:
- A reproducible set of steps exists in repo docs and/or scripts.
- The foiloptimize example can be built and run on Windows with known dependency paths.

title: Mode B test: 3D → XFoil integration + failure path

title: Display control points and bounds boxes in section preview in optimizer panel.
description:
Control Point Distribution (The "Cosine" Spacing)
Do not space control points evenly.

Leading Edge (LE): Cluster more control points near the nose (0% - 20% chord). This is where the pressure gradients are highest and geometry is most sensitive.

Trailing Edge (TE): Fewer points are needed here, mostly to control the "concavity" or "reflex" for pitching moment control.

Guideline: Use 5 to 7 control points for the top surface and 5 to 7 for the bottom.
close_reason: Added control points and bounds boxes visualization to section preview for V1/V3 presets

title: Optimized sections often have bad geometry in the leading edge
description: The optimization often ends with a section where the points in the leading edge form a tangle of some kind. This makes further analysis impossible and it is hard to edit manually. A suggestion is to forbid moving the points near the leading edge to some degree.

comments.author: johe2
comments.text:
Attempted Fixes (Failed):
1. Freezing neighbors (i +/- 1) of the LE point: Resulted in a 'flat area' or 'glued nose' artifact where the LE was stiff but the transition to the optimized section kinked.
2. Tapered Delta + Clamping: Attempted to linearly scale the optimization bound delta from 0 at LE to 100% at 15% chord, while clamping bounds to prevent LE crossover. Resulted in 'tangles' persisting, possibly due to insufficient smoothness constraints or the taper logic allowing too much freedom too soon.
3. Clamping Only: Prevented crossover but didn't solve the curvature discontinuity/kink.

Reverting to original state to rethink strategy. Issue remains open.

comments.author: johe2
comments.text: Added spline monotonic/self-intersection guard in PSOTaskFoil; Test 2 now expects penalty. Headless test run still fails on Test 1 (known). Needs manual verification in GUI.

comments.author: johe2
comments.text: Added stricter LE validation: monotonic/positive thickness checks gated by base, plus LE curvature and turn-angle caps relative to base. Base metrics now computed from optimization base nodes. Please re-test in GUI to see if LE tangle is gone.

comments.author: johe2
comments.text: Resolved by adding LE geometry guards in PSOTaskFoil: reject candidates with negative TE gap, non-monotonic x around LE, self-intersections, negative thickness near LE, and excessive LE curvature/turn-angle relative to base. Base metrics are computed from the optimization base nodes so checks scale with the starting foil.

title: Constraint for thickness x% from LE.
description: Many optimixation runs ends up with a geometry where the last 10% of the section is totally flat. But that is cheating since if that section would be cut off where the thickness ends and then normalized again it would be a less efficient section. So some kind of constraint is needed to keep a manufacturable and realistic section.
close_reason: Added Thick @80% and Thick @90% constraints to prevent flat trailing edges

title: LE geometry almost always bad during and after optimization
description:
This is the root of many other problems. Need some thourough investigation and smart handling. 
Tested with NACA2412 and different numbers of control points. Optimizing for Min Cd at constant Cl of 0.73. Criteria Thickness >= Reference. All LE shapes is concave or tangled. 
Wanted state: When starting with a foil that is somewhat right for the application the leading edge should be kept as intact as possible. Maybe lock it with some fading to 5-10% of the foil. This is also related to the LE criteria not working. And relatied to problems with convergence and finding usable shapes. A bad LE will ruin the xfoil calculations.

description: Add row. Dropdown (Min Drag, Max Lift), Weighting factors

title: PSOTaskFoil should validate polar target selection and handle invalid/empty alpha or Cl specs

title: FoilOptim: variable preset selection plumbing (UI + task)
description: Provide UI + task helpers to choose variable sets and constraints beyond the initial y-only base-node set.

title: FoilOptim: variable preset v1 (y-only base nodes) + constraints
description: Finalize the initial variable set: y-only base-node variables with LE/TE fixed, plus guard constraints (min thickness/no crossover, optional TE gap bounds, optional smoothness/curvature penalties).\n\nSee FOIL_OPTIMIZATION_PLAN.md Phase B.

title: FoilOptim: add preset v2 (camber/thickness or coupled y)
description: Add a second, safer parameterization than raw y-only nodes (e.g., camber+thickness on a fixed x grid, or coupled top/bottom y deltas) to reduce invalid geometries and improve optimization stability.\n\nSee FOIL_OPTIMIZATION_PLAN.md Phase B.
